one_time_description: COVID-19 additional funding received from Indigenous Services Canada and other sources for public health measures, income assistance, and reopening costs
economic_notes: Nation is member of Lesser Slave Lake Indian Regional Council (LSLIRC) which negotiates funding agreements with Government of Canada. Subsidiaries include 327938 Alberta Ltd., Kapawe'no First Nation Narrows Cultural Resort Inc., Kapawe'no First Nation Oil & Gas Company Limited, Kapawe'no First Nation Childcare Society, and Kapawe'no First Nation Wellness Services Society.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 not 2024. Five fully consolidated subsidiaries including an oil & gas company and a cultural resort. Trust funds of $5,273,380 held as financial assets including Ottawa funds held in trust under the Indian Act. Restricted cash includes $153,441 externally restricted by Province of Alberta and $2,160,761 internally restricted for special projects. Large deferred revenue balance of $6,027,624 primarily from LSLIRC and ISC Health. Federal government interest-free loans totalling $319,063 related to land claims. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements. COVID-19 funding of $1,201,598 received during the year. OSR calculated as total revenue minus LSLIRC and ISC-Health and Province of Alberta transfers.
